scope: version-refresh plan for external/openroad-deps; the next OpenROAD rebuild SHOULD apply the bumps listed under deps_to_bump and MUST NOT touch anything listed under deps_to_hold
policy_notes: Current pinned versions read directly from external/openroad-src/etc/DependencyInstaller.sh (lines 48-83 as of 2026-05-20) and cross-checked against build/logs/openroad_deps_prefixes.txt
Boost is pinned via Boost_DIR=external/openroad-deps/lib/cmake/Boost-1.89.0 in openroad_deps_prefixes.txt
DependencyInstaller.sh is NOT modified by this plan; the bumps land as part of the next OpenROAD rebuild change-set

deps_to_bump.name: or-tools
deps_to_bump.rationale: OR-Tools 9.15 ships CP-SAT and routing improvements plus protobuf realignment. Source-compatible API surface with 9.14 for the call sites OpenROAD uses (CP-SAT in dpl/gpl). Low-risk bump, recommended by upstream for SAT-driven detailed placement.

deps_to_bump.name: abseil-cpp
deps_to_bump.rationale: Patch release of the same LTS line (20260107). Upstream OpenROAD PR #10308 (merged) already moved to 20260107.1 in the Bazel-only deps; CMake DependencyInstaller still references .0. Bumping CMake to match keeps Bazel/CMake builds in lock-step and picks up Abseil bugfixes for hash-set and time utilities.
deps_to_bump.checksum_action: recompute ABSL_CHECKSUM for the .1 tarball; do not reuse .0 hash

deps_to_bump.name: pcre2
deps_to_bump.rationale: PCRE2 10.42 (2022) -> 10.47 (2026) closes ~3 years of UTF-8 buffer-handling, JIT compilation, and CVE-class fixes (including CVE-2024-* and CVE-2025-* series). PCRE2 is a transitive dep via SWIG's regex handling; no OpenROAD source change required. This is the staleest dep in the tree and the highest-priority security bump.

deps_to_bump.name: swig
deps_to_bump.rationale: SWIG 4.3.2 ships Tcl-binding fixes (typemap regressions and Tcl 9 compatibility) that directly affect OpenROAD's Tcl shell. 4.3.1->4.3.2 are pure bugfix releases on the 4.3 line; no language/binding behavioral break for the OpenROAD .i files.

deps_to_hold.name: boost
deps_to_hold.reason: Upstream OpenROAD Boost 1.90 PR was closed without merge; 1.90 changes Boost.Geometry and Boost.Polygon headers in ways OpenROAD's odb/gui code has not been adapted to. Hold until upstream reopens and merges.

deps_to_hold.name: spdlog
deps_to_hold.reason: Upstream OpenROAD PR #10104 (spdlog 1.16) was closed; 1.16 changes the formatter API in ways that ripple through Logger/Logger.h call sites. Hold until upstream picks it back up.

deps_to_hold.name: cmake
deps_to_hold.reason: DependencyInstaller hard-pins CMake 3.31 and ships matching aarch64/x86_64 checksums (CMAKE_CHECKSUM_AARCH64, CMAKE_CHECKSUM_X86_64). Bumping requires regenerating both checksums and confirming all CMakeLists.txt minimums; out of scope for a dep-only refresh.

deps_to_hold.name: eigen
deps_to_hold.reason: Eigen 3.4 is the latest stable release; no 3.5 exists. Hold.

deps_to_hold.name: lemon
deps_to_hold.reason: Lemon 1.3.1 is the latest upstream release. No newer line.

deps_to_hold.name: cudd
deps_to_hold.reason: CUDD 3.0.0 is the canonical version used by OpenROAD and OpenSTA; no published successor. Hold.

deps_to_hold.name: gtest
deps_to_hold.reason: Current pin matches OpenROAD upstream; bump only when upstream moves.

deps_to_hold.name: bison
deps_to_hold.reason: Bison 3.8.2 is the OpenROAD upstream pin and matches generated parser fixtures.

deps_to_hold.name: flex
deps_to_hold.reason: Flex 2.6.4 is the long-standing upstream pin; no newer release in 9+ years.

steps: edit external/openroad-src/etc/DependencyInstaller.sh: update the four version+checksum pairs listed under deps_to_bump
recompute MD5 checksums (PCRE, SWIG, ABSL) from the upstream tarballs and update *_CHECKSUM variables
rebuild OpenROAD against the refreshed external/openroad-deps and capture build/logs/openroad_deps_prefixes.txt anew
run the OpenROAD smoke flow (run_openroad.sh on the e1 PD harness) and diff against the prior baseline
execution.rollback: if any bump regresses, revert that single VAR+CHECKSUM pair in DependencyInstaller.sh and rerun -common to restore the prior pin
